crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: NBFC originations drive securitisation volume to first-quarter peak
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The securitisation  market heralded its strongest-ever first-quarter volume with issuances surging 22% on-year to a peak of ~Rs 60,000 crore in April-June 2026, compared with the same period last fiscal (Chart 1 in annexure).

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Specialty chemical makers set to catalyse ~6% revenue growth
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Revenue growth of specialty chemical manufacturers is expected to moderate 200 bps  this fiscal from ~8% each in the past two fiscals. Firm domestic demand will support growth but export weakness—driven by supply disruptions and cautious overseas procurement—will cap the overall pace, with trade flo

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: For MFIs, growth gathering pace, AUM to surge ~20% this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Growth in assets under management (AUM) of microfinance institutions (MFIs)  is set to accelerate to ~20% this fiscal from 4% last fiscal. Their AUM comprises two businesses, primarily—the microfinance portfolio and non-microfinance.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: AUM growth of affordable-housing lenders seen steady at 19-20%
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Growth in assets under management (AUM) of affordable housing finance companies  (A-HFCs) is expected to remain healthy at 19-20% this fiscal and the next, in line with the ~19% growth last fiscal. (Chart 1 in annexure).

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Dairies to churn out 13-15% revenue growth on sustained demand
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Revenue growth of the organised dairy sector is expected to accelerate by 200–400 basis points this fiscal, over a healthy print of ~11% growth estimated for last fiscal. This uptick will be supported by a sustained volume growth of 8–10% and staggered price increases. Volume growth will be driven b

crisil-research.title: RRR: Roti Rice Rate
crisil-research.description: The average cost of preparing a thali at home is calculated based on input prices prevailing in north, south, east and west India. The monthly change reflects the impact on the common man’s expenditure. The data also reveals the ingredients (cereals, pulses, broilers, vegetables, spices, edible oil

crisil-research.title: SectorVector - Housing hotspots
crisil-research.description: Tier 21 cities saw demand for residential real estate clock a 14% compound annual growth rate between fiscals 2021 and 2026, with the likes of Nagpur, Coimbatore and Lucknow surging at a much faster 20%.

crisil-research.title: Fundamentals fine, but cracks show
crisil-research.description: The global market for senior housing has had a mixed outing this past year. Though fundamentals such as occupancy and absorption have improved amid muted new supply, high profile sales at a loss and broader rationalization of weaker assets have underscored operating and financing risks, especially f

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: IIP momentum sustains
crisil-research.description: Industrial growth, as measured by the Index of Industrial Production (IIP), grew to 5.1% in May from 4.9% in April, above the fiscal 2026 average of 4.3%. Among the headline sectors, electricity and gas was the primary driver of growth on-year (9.9% vs 4.6%). Growth in manufacturing (5.5% vs 6.1%) a

crisil-research.title: Implementing standardized credit risk assessment for bank exposures under Basel III
crisil-research.description: A large European bank was preparing to implement standardized credit risk assessment (SCRA) requirements as part of the EU Banking Package (Capital Requirements Regulation (CRR III) / Capital Requirements Directive (CRD VI) regulatory framework). The bank had significant exposure to a diverse set of

crisil-research.title: Banking Sentimeter
crisil-research.description: India’s banking sector recorded ~13.5% YoY deposit growth in fiscal 2026, however, credit growth remained higher, keeping the CD ratio above 81%. This underscores the need for stronger liability mobilisation. While banks are relying more on term deposits, CASA ratios have declined (~39%) due to cust

baroda-etrade-str.title: Building Materials: Q4FY26 Review – Pipes drive earnings growth; wood panels sustain momentum

ssga-insights.title: ETF inflows set records in first half
ssga-insights.description: Record ETF inflows topped $1 trillion in H1 2026 as investors favored active ETFs, bonds, technology, and emerging markets amid evolving macro risks.

ssga-insights.title: Make room for ALLW
ssga-insights.description: Learn how to fund allocations to the State Street® Bridgewater® All Weather® ETF from equities, bonds, or both to improve portfolio diversification and balance risk.

ssga-insights.title: From anchor to growth tailwind
ssga-insights.description: Now investors can add more exposure to growth in the core for less with the State Street® SPDR® Portfolio Nasdaq® 100 ETF.

ssga-insights.title: Is it time to rethink the 60/40 portfolio?
ssga-insights.description: Explore why it may be time to look beyond the 60/40 portfolio, and how alternative ETFs can offer a fresh way to diversify and strengthen portfolios for the future.

ssga-insights.title: Our Annual Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) Report is Here
ssga-insights.description: Read our annual report on climate-change risk analysis and management.

ssga-insights.title: What is the Nasdaq and how does it drive the modern global economy?
ssga-insights.description: What is the Nasdaq? More than an exchange or index, it reflects growth leadership and the companies shaping today’s—and tomorrow’s—economies. See why.

ssga-insights.title: Canada at a crossroads
ssga-insights.description: Canada’s outlook is fragile and tied to USMCA negotiations, with widening gaps between trade‑sensitive and domestic sectors as uncertainty clears or persists.

ssga-insights.title: A case for investing in innovation
ssga-insights.description: Despite the unprecedented impact of technological breakthroughs, many portfolios lack exposure to innovation. Learn to identify and capture the economic benefits of innovation.

ssga-insights.title: ETFs on the rise: The investment choice revolutionizing portfolios
ssga-insights.description: ETFs are now considered key building blocks within many portfolios. Find out why they’re attracting continued attention from retail and institutional investors alike.

ssga-insights.title: Small caps gain amid resilient US growth
ssga-insights.description: Resilient US growth is driving small-cap outperformance, with the Russell 2000 leading amid cyclical trends, sector shifts, and evolving valuation dynamics.

ssga-insights.title: Defined Contribution Insights
ssga-insights.description: Explore our latest insights on defined contribution, from investment research and innovation to retirement income, public policy and participant engagement.

ssga-insights.title: Investing in real assets with ETFs
ssga-insights.description: Consider whether real assets like gold, commodities, and infrastructure can improve the diversification of your 60/40 portfolio—especially now that bonds are no longer the go-to for offsetting stock volatility.

ssga-insights.title: Growth holds but inflation risks reshape outlook
ssga-insights.description: Global growth steadies as inflation risks persist, with AI-driven demand, geopolitics, and policy uncertainty shaping outlooks across major economies and markets.

ssga-insights.title: UK politics: Continuity priced in, risks simmer
ssga-insights.description: Markets show little reaction to Starmer’s exit, pricing continuity. However, internal Labour Party dynamics suggest a less attractive policy mix ahead.

ssga-insights.title: Navigating rising market concentration with Enhanced strategies
ssga-insights.description: Market concentration is near historic highs, raising concerns that core equity allocations are becoming increasingly reliant on a narrow set of themes and names. We explore how Enhanced equity strategies may offer a disciplined, risk-aware complement.

ssga-insights.title: Core portfolio construction principles
ssga-insights.description: What four principles do investors need to consider when it comes to core portfolio construction? Learn about what makes a strong foundation for a flexible portfolio from Matthew Bartolini.

ssga-insights.title: Dissecting equity momentum
ssga-insights.description: Momentum has been the main driver of 2026 equity factor returns, particularly in Technology. But diversification remains essential as market leadership evolves.

ssga-insights.title: Hawkish Fed signals drive market caution
ssga-insights.description: Hawkish signals from the Fed, steady UK policy, and Japan’s rate hike highlight a cautious global outlook amid inflation risks and evolving fiscal uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: Real assets: diversification through conflict
ssga-insights.description: Real assets show diverging correlations during conflict, reinforcing their role as dynamic diversifiers as inflation, rates, and geopolitical shocks reshape portfolios.

ssga-insights.title: ETF Impact Report 2026-2027
ssga-insights.description: What’s next for today’s dynamic ETF market? Get our ETF Impact Report 2026-2027 to uncover top trends and our predictions for the future.

ssga-insights.title: How macro fragmentation may reshape core bond benchmarks
ssga-insights.description: Macro fragmentation is reshaping bond benchmarks. Explore how rising deficits, higher term premiums, and growing Treasury weight reshape core fixed income investing.

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market Starter
icici-bank-research.description: India’s wholesale price inflation (WPI) accelerated to a 42-month high of 8.3% YoY in April from 3.88% YoY in March, driven by a sharp rise in crude oil, natural gas, food and fuel prices alongside a favourable base effect. Read the report to know more.

ing-think-rss.title: Oil off its highs but risks return
ing-think-rss.description: Oil prices came under significant pressure following the temporary deal between the US and Iran. But the recent flare-up in hostilities poses risks. The key factors for the outlook remain the sustainability of the Persian Gulf supply recovery and how soon Chinese buying returns to the market

ing-think-rss.title: Our latest views on the major central banks
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: Our take on what could be next for the Federal Reserve, the European Central Bank and the Bank of England over the coming months

ing-think-rss.title: Slowing US inflation to keep the Fed on hold
ing-think-rss.description: NORTH AMERICA: Lower motor fuel prices are bringing relief for consumers and suggest that inflation may have peaked. Slowing housing rents, weak wage growth and a waning influence from tariffs should more than offset concerns tied to tech-related inflation pressures. With the jobs market offering li

ing-think-rss.title: A fragile reprieve for the eurozone
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: As a net energy importer, lower oil prices are clearly favourable for the eurozone. A Goldilocks scenario – where inflation moderates and growth picks up again – remains the baseline, although renewed Middle East tensions have increased the risks around that outlook

ing-think-rss.title: 3 key questions for China’s second half of 2026
ing-think-rss.description: ASIA/PACIFIC: China’s economy has seen a growing divergence this year, with two distinct K-shaped trends moving in opposite directions. ing-think-rss.title: Lower oil prices a boon for Britain, Burnham and the Bank of England
ing-think-rss.description: UNITED KINGDOM: UK inflation is unlikely to peak much above 3% this year, taking some of the pressure off the public finances and the Bank of England to hike interest rates

ing-think-rss.title: Oil prices still offer relief for Asia, but no policy pivot
ing-think-rss.description: With oil prices still below their recent peaks, inflationary and external pressures across Asia have eased somewhat. But second-order inflation effects and FX vulnerabilities remain key constraints. Central banks are likely to maintain a tightening bias until price pressures and external balances i

ing-think-rss.title: CEE: Inflation relief reshapes the rates outlook
ing-think-rss.description: CENTRAL AND EASTERN EUROPE: Central and Eastern Europe starts the second half of the year with softer inflation and a more dovish rates outlook. Poland has shifted from rate hike fears to speculation about cuts; Czech rates are likely to stay on hold, while Hungary offers the clearest recovery story

ing-think-rss.title: CIS-4 resilience to face tests in the second half of 2026
ing-think-rss.description: COMMONWEALTH OF INDEPENDENT STATES: Amid volatility in global commodity markets, the CIS-4 is starting the second half of the year with stronger financial buffers, stable FX and resilient demand, but inflation risks remain sticky. Foreign-policy spillovers, rate path uncertainty and market access wi

ing-think-rss.title: FX: Dollar dances in the dark
ing-think-rss.description: The dollar has been enjoying the hawkish reassessment of Fed policy over the last couple of months. In practice, however, there hasn’t been much communication from the Fed at all now that Kevin Warsh is stepping back from forward guidance. If we’re right with our Fed call of unchanged policy, the d

goldman-sachs-reports.title: UK Social Mobility: Remains a Tough Climb but One Worth Pursuing
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Europe] Increasing social mobility, an important objective in its own right, would also likely have economic benefits, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The Outlook for Data Centers in Asia
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] Traditional cloud workloads as well as new AI-first demand are driving growth in data centers in Asia, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Private Markets Are Expected to Have a Growing Role in Data Center Financing
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] With planned hyperscaler spending on AI and data centers projected to top $5 trillion by 2030, capital from private market infrastructure and real estate funds will become increasingly important for the AI buildout.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: HALO Stocks in Emerging Markets Could Extend Rally as Heavy Industries Surge
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Emerging-market companies marked by heavy assets and low obsolescence, or HALO, are benefiting as the AI boom drives demand for infrastructure.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why Oil Prices Could ‘Grind Lower’ Amid the US-Iran Deal
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Energy] While the recent deal between the US and Iran is likely already reflected in the oil market, prices are likely to gradually decline over time, says Jerome Dortmans, co-head of Global Oil and Products Trading in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: South Korea's Growing Role in Humanoid Robot Development
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] While humans still outperform humanoid robots on basic real-world tasks, the race is on to improve the machines. South Korea is well positioned to play a key role in the supply chain and humanoid adoptions, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: GS SUSTAIN: How Aging and Declining Populations Will Drive Product and Company Demand
goldman-sachs-reports.description: Goldman Sachs Research’s framework identifies the sectors that are expected to have the greatest increase in demand as a result of aging populations.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Japan’s Strategic Awakening
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Geopolitics] Japan is at an inflection point as geopolitics and technology transform the global landscape.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: An AI Job Apocalypse?
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] Rapid improvements in AI capabilities and growing corporate adoption have led to predictions that the technology could lead to large-scale job losses before the end of the decade. So, just how concerned should we be about an AI “job apocalypse”?

jpmorgan-research.title: US–Israel military operation against Iran: Are markets on edge?
jpmorgan-research.description: The conflict could pose an upside risk for oil, gold and aluminum prices — though uncertainties around its longer-term impact remain.
